intro: The clearest split is that Novu carries low lock-in and Batch carries medium lock-in. Novu is Open-source notification infrastructure with multi-channel support, in-app feed, user preferences, and self-hostable backend; Batch is Mobile push notification and in-app messaging platform with segmentation, A/B testing, and transactional message. The data points Novu at mobile app engagement and user lifecycle messaging, and Batch at transactional + marketing push with behavioral segmentation and a/b testing push notification copy and send-time. Pricing: Novu is freemium, free tier; Batch is freemium, free tier. Lock-in is low for Novu and medium for Batch, with DX at 5/5 vs 4/5 and transparency at 5/5 vs 4/5. Only Novu lists an enterprise tier in the data. Honest trade-off: Novu is not for web-only products without mobile; Batch is not for teams needing email, sms, and in-app in one mature suite, so confirm the fit first, then weigh the pivotal factor above against your budget and lock-in tolerance.
quick_take: Novu is for mobile; Batch is for european mobile apps wanting gdpr-native push with segmentation; decide based on how much lock-in you tolerate.
verdict_a: Choose Novu if you prefer low lock-in and easier exit and your workload matches mobile. Skip it if your context is web-only products without mobile.
verdict_b: Choose Batch if you can accept medium lock-in in exchange for scope and your workload matches european mobile apps wanting gdpr-native push with segmentation. Skip it if your context is teams needing email, sms, and in-app in one mature suite.

faq.a: Entry pricing is listed as $0 for Novu and unknown for Batch. Both offer a free tier for getting started. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Novu to Batch?
faq.a: Novu is tagged with low lock-in (oss notification infrastructure, self-hostable). Batch is tagged with medium lock-in (push/messaging sdk integration). Plan for SDK replacement, data export, and a parallel-run period; the dataset does not include migration tooling details.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Novu or Batch?
faq.a: The dataset rates Novu at 5/5 for developer experience and Batch at 4/5, so Novu scores higher. That score reflects overall polish rather than any single integration, so trial both against your stack before committing.

faq.q: Is Batch a good alternative to Novu?
faq.a: Batch is listed as fitting european mobile apps wanting gdpr-native push with segmentation, which overlaps with but is not identical to Novu's scope. Batch is flagged as not for teams needing email, sms, and in-app in one mature suite, so confirm your context does not fall there.
